characters.name: Bobo MacDonald
characters.description: Margaret "Bobo" MacDonaldDate of birth1904Date of death22 September 1993 (Aged 89)OccupationDresserNannyRelationshipsFamilyRuby MacDonald (Sister)CreditsPlayed byLizzie McInnernySeason(s)1.2First appearanceWolferton SplashFinal appearanceLisbonMargaret "Bobo" MacDonaldLVO RVM is a recurring character inSeasons 1and2of the Netflix series,The Crown.  She serves asQueen Elizabeth II'snanny and later as her dresser and confidante. She is portrayed byLizzy McInnerny. She started off as a nursemaid to baby Elizabeth, and the two shared a bedroom until Elizabeth was 11 years old. Bobo and Elizabeth were such close friends that some say “Bobo” was Elizabeth’s first word. As the Elizabeth grew up and later became queen, Bobo went on to become her dresser. She served Elizabeth II for 67 years and in her later years, when she no longer had any royal duties, she stayed at Buckingham Palace as the Queen’s personal friend. She died on September 22, 1993, in her suite at Buckingham Palace at the age of 89. Elizabeth II attended her memorial at The Queen's Chapel, St. James's Palace.

characters.name: Carole Middleton
characters.description: Carole MiddletonOccupationBusinesswomanRelationshipsMarital statusMarriedSpouseMichael MiddletonChildrenCatherine MiddletonPippa MiddletonJames MiddletonCreditsPlayed byEve BestSeason(s)6First appearanceAlma MaterFinal appearanceHope Street Carole Middleton(néeCarole Elizabeth Goldsmith) is the mother ofCatherine Middletonand later the mother-in law toPrince William. She appears inSeason 6ofThe Crown. InThe Crown, she is portrayed byEve Best. Carole is a British businesswoman. She is the mother of Catherine, Pippa, and James Middleton. Carole strongly encouraged her daughter Kate to connect with William. Some, including Kate, even suspected her mother of encouraging Kate to take a gap year to match Will's plans. Carole was delighted when William and Kate got together. She hosted him at a viewing of the Queen's Jubilee.

characters.name: Charles, Prince of Wales
characters.actor_name: Billy Jenkins(child, Season 1-2)Julian Baring(preteen, Season 2)Josh O'Connor(Seasons 3-4)Dominic West(Seasons 5-6)
characters.description: Charles, Prince of WalesSeason 5-6Season 3-4Title(s)HRH Prince Charles of EdinburghHRH The Duke of CornwallHRH The Prince of WalesHM The KingBirth nameCharles Philip Arthur GeorgeDate of birthNovember 14, 1948BirthplaceBuckingham Palace, London, EnglandAge0—6(Season 1)7—16(Season 2)16—28(Season 3)30-42(Season 4)42-48(Season 5)49-54(Season 6)Nickname(s)Fred(byCamilla)Eeyore(byAnne)Bloody Weak(byPhilip)Little Pa Peep(by himself andhissons)SpouseLady Diana Spencer(m. 1981, div. 1996)Camilla Parker Bowles(m.2005-)ChildrenPrince William, Prince of WalesPrince Harry, Duke of SussexCreditsPlayed byBilly Jenkins(child, Season 1-2)Julian Baring(preteen, Season 2)Josh O'Connor(Seasons 3-4)Dominic West(Seasons 5-6)Season(s)1•2•3•4•5•6First appearance“Wolferton Splash”Final appearance“Sleep Dearie Sleep” Charles, Prince of Wales(bornCharles Philip Arthur George Mountbatten-Windsor, and later known asHis Majesty The King) is the son ofQueen Elizabeth IIand the heir to the throne. InThe CrownNetflix series, he is portrayed by Billy Jenkins and Julian Baring as a child and adolescent, byJosh O'Connorin Seasons 3–4, and byDominic Westin Seasons 5–6. Throughout the series, Prince Charles is the heir apparent to the British throne as the oldest son ofQueen Elizabeth IIandPrince Philip, Duke of Edinburgh. He served Duke of Cornwall and Duke of Rothesay since 1952, and he is the oldest and longest-serving heir apparent in British history.

characters.name: Derek "Dazzle" Jennings
characters.description: Derek "Dazzle" JenningsDate of birth1946Date of death1995OccupationCivil ServantPriestRelationshipsMarital statusBachelorFriendsPrincess MargaretCreditsPlayed byTom BurkeSeason(s)4Appearance“The Hereditary Principle”Derek "Dazzle" Jenningsis a guest character inSeason 4of the Netflix series,The Crown. He is a British senior civil servant and later a Catholic priest who briefly shares an intimate flirtatious friendship withPrincess Margaret. He is portrayed byTom Burke. Derek Jennings was born in 1946 and worked as a senior civil servant in the British Government's Department of the Environment. In 1984, he quit his job to take the Holy Orders and become a Catholic priest. Jennings was gay.

characters.name: Diana, Princess of Wales
characters.description: Diana, Princess of WalesSeason 6Season 5Season 4Title(s)The Honourable Diana SpencerLady Diana SpencerHRH The Princess of WalesDiana, Princess of WalesBirth nameDiana Frances SpencerDate of birthJuly 1, 1961BirthplacePark House, Sandringham, EnglandDate of deathAugust 31, 1997 (aged 36)Pitié-Salpêtrière Hospital, Paris, FranceAge18-29(Season 4)30-34(Season 5)35-36(Season 6)Nickname(s)Barbie(by Camilla, in real life)Mummy(byhersons)Lady DiShy DiDuch(childhood nickname)The People's Princess(posthumous, byTony Blair)SpouseCharles, Prince of Wales(m. 1981; div. 1996)ChildrenHRH William, Prince of WalesPrince Harry, Duke of SussexCreditsPlayed byEmma Corrin(Season 4)Elizabeth Debicki(Season 5, 6)Season(s)4•5•6First appearance“Gold Stick”Final appearance“Dis-Moi Oui”(death)“Alma Mater”(flashback) Diana, Princess of Wales(born LadyDiana Frances Spencer), was a member of the British Royal Family and the first wife ofCharles, Prince of Wales, the heir apparent to the British throne. She is also the mother ofPrince WilliamandPrince Harry. InThe Crown, she is portrayed byEmma Corrinin Season 4 and byElizabeth Debickiin Seasons 5 and 6. Diana was born the fourth of five children to John Spencer, Viscount Althorp (1924-1992), and Frances Spencer, Viscountess Althorp (1936-2004). She has four siblings: John (January 12,1960; Stillborn) and Charles (May 20, 1964) and Elizabeth "Sarah" (March 19, 1955) and Cynthia "Jane" (February 11, 1957). The Spencer family were an aristocratic family, who had been allied with the British royal family for generations. Indeed, Lady Fermoy, served as lady-in-waiting tothe Queen Mother. In her youth she Diana was known informally as "Duch", in reference to her 'duchess'-like attitude and early aspirations to marry into the royal family. Descriptions of the real Princess of Wales are often polarised. However, Diana is often noted for her compassion, style, charisma, and her devotion to her sons Prince William and Prince Harry. Many people have often described her as someone who was "led by her heart". According to Tina Brown, in her early years Diana possessed a "power" - shared with the Queen Mother - a charisma that would allow her to instinctively use her appeal to achieve her goals. Diana was also not without criticism, some described her as "unpredictable, egocentric, and possessive", and also manipulative of people and situations around her. It is also well known that Diana seriously struggled with her mental health, struggling with Bulimia, and depression. The Crowndepicts Princess Diana as a complicated young woman who goes through change throughout Season Four. At the outset, Diana is a kindhearted, inexperienced, naïve, and charismatic teenager. It is also implied that Diana is manipulative and immature to an extent. Throughout the season, Diana grows into a devoted and caring mother, and struggles immensely with her position in the royal family and with her doomed marriage to Prince Charles. Diana also begins her well-
characters.personality_section: Descriptions of the real Princess of Wales are often polarised. However, Diana is often noted for her compassion, style, charisma, and her devotion to her sons Prince William and Prince Harry. Many people have often described her as someone who was "led by her heart". According to Tina Brown, in her early years Diana possessed a "power" - shared with the Queen Mother - a charisma that would allow her to instinctively use her appeal to achieve her goals. Diana was also not without criticism, some described her as "unpredictable, egocentric, and possessive", and also manipulative of people and situations around her. It is also well known that Diana seriously struggled with her mental health, struggling with Bulimia, and depression. The Crowndepicts Princess Diana as a complicated young woman who goes through change throughout Season Four. At the outset, Diana is a kindhearted, inexperienced, naïve, and charismatic teenager. It is also implied that Diana is manipulative and immature to an extent. Throughout the season, Diana grows into a devoted and caring mother, and struggles immensely with her position in the royal family and with her doomed marriage to Prince Charles. Diana also begins her well-known charity work.

Diana was also not without criticism, some described her as "unpredictable, egocentric, and possessive", and also manipulative of people and situations around her. It is also well known that Diana seriously struggled with her mental health, struggling with Bulimia, and depression. The Crowndepicts Princess Diana as a complicated young woman who goes through change throughout Season Four. At the outset, Diana is a kindhearted, inexperienced, naïve, and charismatic teenager. It is also implied that Diana is manipulative and immature to an extent. Throughout the season, Diana grows into a devoted and caring mother, and struggles immensely with her position in the royal family and with her doomed marriage to Prince Charles. Diana also begins her well-
characters.personality_section: Descriptions of the real Princess of Wales are often polarised. However, Diana is often noted for her compassion, style, charisma, and her devotion to her sons Prince William and Prince Harry. Many people have often described her as someone who was "led by her heart". According to Tina Brown, in her early years Diana possessed a "power" - shared with the Queen Mother - a charisma that would allow her to instinctively use her appeal to achieve her goals. Diana was also not without criticism, some described her as "unpredictable, egocentric, and possessive", and also manipulative of people and situations around her. It is also well known that Diana seriously struggled with her mental health, struggling with Bulimia, and depression. The Crowndepicts Princess Diana as a complicated young woman who goes through change throughout Season Four. At the outset, Diana is a kindhearted, inexperienced, naïve, and charismatic teenager. It is also implied that Diana is manipulative and immature to an extent. Throughout the season, Diana grows into a devoted and caring mother, and struggles immensely with her position in the royal family and with her doomed marriage to Prince Charles. Diana also begins her well-known charity work.

characters.name: Edward Millward
characters.description: Edward "Tedi" MillwardDate of birth28 June 1930Date of death18 April 2020 (Aged 89)OccupationPolitical ActivistRelationshipsMarital statusMarriedSpouseSilvia HartChildren2CreditsPlayed byMark Lewis JonesSeason(s)3Appearance“Tywysog Cymru” Edward "Tedi" Millwardis a guest character inSeason 3of the Netflix series,The Crown. He is a Welsh nationalist who serves as Vice-President of Plaid Cymru from 1966 until 1968 and founder of the Welsh Language Society. In 1969, he serves asPrince Charles'sWelsh language tutor prior to his investiture as Prince of Wales. He is portrayed by Mark Lewis Jones inSeason 3, episode 6.
